question: What are the trade-offs between autonomous agents and workflow-based agents?

claim_lines: Autonomous agents are the better fit when the task is open-ended, the number of steps cannot be predicted, and dynamic tool use matters; the trade-off is higher cost, more variance, and more need for guardrails and trust. Workflow-based agents are the better fit when you can predefine steps and want predictability, monitoring, replay, retries, and human approval. That conclusion is a synthesis of the sources below, not a universal benchmark result. https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/concepts/workflows/ https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/durable-execution

- **flexibility** — Agents are more flexible because they can adapt their plan and tool use to intermediate results; workflows are limited by predefined code paths. https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/concepts/what-are-agents/ https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents

- **controllability** — Workflows are more controllable because they support pause/resume/restart, approvals, and explicit step boundaries; agents require more trust and guardrails. https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/concepts/workflows/ https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents

- **observability** — Workflows and agent runtimes with tracing are easier to inspect because you can record step-by-step progress, tool calls, and decision points. https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/observability https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/concepts/workflows/

- **cost** — Autonomous agents usually cost more because they can run many turns; workflows can route simple work to smaller, cheaper models. https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents

- **reproducibility** — Workflow engines with checkpointing and replay are more reproducible because completed steps are persisted and runs can be replayed. https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/durable-execution

- **failure recovery** — Workflows recover better from interruptions because they retry failed steps and resume from the last successful checkpoint/state. https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/concepts/workflows/ https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/durable-execution
